task.title: Add role/worktree isolation metadata for claims and validation
task.summary: Expose builder/validator/publisher roles, worktree/SHA metadata, and validation isolation evidence through CodeWiki claims, validation reports, graph views, and tests.

goal.outcome: Claims remain logical leases, while active builder/validator/publisher sessions and validation reports can record worktree and exact Git SHA evidence; graph/status consumers can inspect this metadata without treating it as canonical filesystem truth.
acceptance: codewiki_claim accepts and persists role/worktree metadata for builder, validator, publisher, and observer claims.
codewiki_validation accepts and writes isolation evidence including fresh_context, worktree_path, branch, base/head/validated/published SHA fields, clean status, and related claim/session refs.
Generated graph exposes claim role/worktree metadata and validation isolation metadata in compact nodes/views.
Implementation/task-close validation reports with missing isolation metadata remain backward-compatible but can be distinguished from isolated validation evidence.
Tests cover claim normalization, validation report writing, and graph visibility for role/worktree/SHA isolation fields.
non_goals: Do not require one worktree per claim.
Do not auto-spawn worktrees or sessions.
Do not make claim metadata a security boundary or filesystem source of truth.
Do not retroactively invalidate legacy validation reports.

summary: Implemented role/worktree/SHA isolation metadata for claims, validation reports, graph views, and tests.

evidence: Added focused smoke coverage for metadata persistence and graph projection instead of broad UI or filesystem spawning tests.
Kept legacy validation compatibility by testing positive isolated path and leaving old reports accepted.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Shared types and Pi schemas now expose claim roles, worktree metadata, and validation isolation input.
Claims normalize/persist role and worktree metadata; validation reports normalize/persist isolation metadata.
Graph emits compact role/isolation nodes and views.
notes: Claim metadata remains coordination metadata, not filesystem truth or a security boundary.
Builder context for this implementation is current session; future validator reports can record fresh worktree and validated_sha via new isolation fields.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Added focused smoke coverage for metadata persistence and graph projection instead of broad UI or filesystem spawning tests.
Kept legacy validation compatibility by testing positive isolated path and leaving old reports accepted.
code_change_evidence: Shared types and Pi schemas now expose claim roles, worktree metadata, and validation isolation input.
Claims normalize/persist role and worktree metadata; validation reports normalize/persist isolation metadata.
Graph emits compact role/isolation nodes and views.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_claim accepts and persists role/worktree metadata for builder, validator, publisher, and observer claims.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: CodewikiClaimToolInput, TypeBox schema, claims normalizer, and smoke test cover role + worktree metadata persistence.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: codewiki_validation accepts and writes isolation evidence including fresh_context, worktree_path, branch, base/head/validated/published SHA fields, clean status, and related claim/session refs.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: CodewikiValidationReportInput/schema and writeValidationReport persist normalized isolation evidence; smoke test asserts validator metadata in report JSON.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Generated graph exposes claim role/worktree metadata and validation isolation metadata in compact nodes/views.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts adds role/worktree to claim nodes, isolation_status/isolation to validation nodes, and views.claims/views.validation isolation rows; smoke test asserts graph output.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Implementation/task-close validation reports with missing isolation metadata remain backward-compatible but can be distinguished from isolated validation evidence.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: graph.ts classifies validation isolation as legacy, partial, or isolated without routing legacy pass reports as failures.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tests cover claim normalization, validation report writing, and graph visibility for role/worktree/SHA isolation fields.

closure_brief.user_intent: Make CodeWiki explicit about using worktrees for state isolation, fresh validation sessions for judgment isolation, and SHAs for exact Git identities.
implemented_changes: Added role/worktree/SHA metadata to claims and schemas.
Added validation isolation metadata to validation reports and schemas.
Added graph nodes/views for claim and validation isolation status.
Added smoke tests for persistence and graph visibility.
Updated system docs and roadmap task evidence.
acceptance_evidence: All TASK-070 acceptance criteria mapped in implementation build acceptance_mapping.
non_goals_preserved: Claim metadata is not a security boundary.
remaining_risks: Automated enforcement/spawn orchestration can be added later if policy demands it.

risks: Fresh validator and publisher worktrees are now representable but not automatically enforced or spawned.

commit.title: chore(codewiki): record TASK-070 Add role/worktree isolation metadata for claims and validation implementation evidence
commit.body:
Implemented role/worktree/SHA isolation metadata for claims, validation reports, graph views, and tests.

Checks: npm run typecheck, npm run test:features, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm run test:pack, npm test
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
